sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: HDFC Bank shares will bounce back in FY27, CLSA says projecting 28% upside
feed.summary: CLSA has maintained an 'Outperform' rating on HDFC Bank, projecting a 28% upside with a price target of ₹1,200. The brokerage firm believes current concerns about moderated deposit growth and the rising loan-to-deposit ratio are either misconceived or temporary, expecting FY27 to be a bounce-back year for the bank. Despite recent stock corrections, CLSA sees this as an opportune time for investors to buy into the well-established franchise, especially with the bank trading at a discount compared to ICICI Bank.

feed.title: Jazz Pharmaceuticals stock rating reiterated at Outperform by RBC Capital
feed.summary: RBC Capital has reaffirmed its Outperform rating for Jazz Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:JAZZ) with a price target of $194.00, citing that the market's reaction to recent zanidatamab data "misses the broader point" of the drug's effectiveness and commercial potential. Despite a recent stock price dip, the firm maintains its outlook for over $900 million in peak U.S. sales for zanidatamab in GEA, with the company showing strong momentum and positive developments in other drug trials. Other analysts like Piper Sandler and BofA Securities have also raised price targets following positive HERIZON-GEA-01 study results, though UBS downgraded the stock to Neutral.

feed.title: Aviation Capital orders 50 Boeing 737 MAX jets

feed.title: Honda to adopt new H mark
feed.summary: Honda is set to introduce a redesigned 'H mark' from 2027, which will feature on its electric and major hybrid-electric models, including the new Honda 0 Series. This updated emblem, symbolizing two outstretched hands, will also be integrated across its business operations, such as dealerships and motorsport activities. The change signifies the arrival of Honda's next-generation EVs and its global commitment.

feed.title: Expanded Data Center Load Strategy Could Be A Game Changer For Xcel Energy (XEL)
feed.summary: Xcel Energy is expanding its capital investment plan to US$60 billion, driven significantly by anticipated data center demand, which is expected to account for 60% of retail sales growth through 2030. This strategy, while reinforcing growth, also intensifies funding risks and potential equity dilution. The company aims to secure long-term pricing with data centers to manage costs and sustain its dividend amid these infrastructure investments.

feed.title: Mattel adds an autistic Barbie to doll line devoted to showcasing diversity and inclusion
feed.summary: Mattel is introducing an autistic Barbie doll as part of its ongoing commitment to diversity and inclusion in its Fashionistas line. Developed in partnership with the Autistic Self Advocacy Network, the doll incorporates various subtle features to reflect how autistic individuals experience the world, such as avoiding direct eye contact and articulated limbs for stimming. This new addition follows other inclusive Barbies, including those with Down syndrome, vitiligo, and prosthetic limbs, aiming to represent the world's diverse children.

feed.title: Novartis, SciNeuro deal offers hope for 55 million suffering from Alzheimer’s
feed.summary: A US$1.7 billion partnership between China's SciNeuro Pharmaceuticals and Swiss giant Novartis aims to develop treatments for Alzheimer's disease, which affects 55 million people globally. Novartis gains exclusive worldwide rights to SciNeuro’s novel amyloid beta targeted antibody program, which uses proprietary blood-brain barrier shuttle technology. SciNeuro will receive an upfront payment of US$165 million and could earn up to US$1.5 billion in milestones and royalties.

feed.title: After-hours INFY (Infosys Limited) NYSE $17.79 ahead of Jan 14 earnings: what to watch
feed.summary: Infosys Limited (INFY) is trading at $17.79 on the NYSE ahead of its Q4 earnings report on January 14, 2026. Investors are closely watching revenue mix, margin performance, and guidance related to generative AI deals. Meyka AI assigns INFY a "BUY" grade (83.64 Grade A) but projects a short-term downside to $16.78 and a longer-term downside to $13.27, stressing the importance of clear AI-driven revenue growth to justify its premium valuation.

feed.title: Lantern Pharma establishes AI Centre of Excellence and Advanced Agentic Labs in Bengaluru
feed.summary: US-based Lantern Pharma Inc., a company focused on AI-driven precision oncology, has announced the opening of an AI Centre of Excellence and Advanced Agentic Labs in Bengaluru, India. This initiative aims to scale up Lantern's AI platform globally, expanding its computational capacity and technical talent. The center will support both Lantern's internal pipeline and collaborations, enhancing its proprietary RADR® AI platform and accelerating the development of specialized large language models and quantitative models for drug discovery.

feed.title: What Global Net Lease (GNL)'s Post-Restructuring Dividend Declaration Means For Shareholders
feed.summary: Global Net Lease (GNL) has declared a US$0.190 per share dividend for Q1 2026, following a major restructuring that included a US$3.30 billion asset disposition and a US$2.70 billion debt reduction. This move impacts the company's investment narrative, emphasizing a shift to a simpler single-tenant net lease portfolio and a cleaner balance sheet to support the dividend and improve financial resilience. While the dividend affirmation doesn't change the near-term picture significantly, the focus remains on whether earnings can comfortably cover the payout, especially as asset base reduction could pressure future AFFO.

Meyka AI rates RF with a score of 78.14 (B+, BUY) and analysts have a consensus target of $28.93, with key risks including regional bank lending pressure and sensitivity to Fed moves.

feed.title: A Look At Charles River Laboratories (CRL) Valuation As CEO Succession Plan Takes Shape
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories International (CRL) is undergoing a leadership transition with CEO James C. Foster retiring in May 2026 and Birgit Girshick becoming his successor. Following a strong period for its shares, the article examines whether CRL is overvalued, noting that a popular narrative suggests it's 15.8% overvalued at $218.82 compared to a fair value of $188.93, while another view based on P/S ratios suggests modest value support. The company's strategic focus on cost reduction and disciplined capital allocation aims to strengthen long-term earnings and free cash flow generation.

feed.title: Aviation Capital Group Orders 50 Boeing 737 MAX Jets
feed.summary: Aviation Capital Group (ACG) has placed a new order for 50 Boeing 737 MAX jets, including 25 737-8 and 25 737-10 airplanes, doubling its 737-10 order book. This acquisition brings ACG's total Boeing 737 MAX order book to 121 aircraft, making it the lessor with the largest order for the 737-10. This move is part of ACG's growth strategy to offer fuel-efficient aircraft technology and support its global customers' expansion.

feed.title: PG&E’s Match My Payment Bill-Pay Program Extended, Funds Are Limited
feed.summary: PG&E has extended its Match My Payment Program into 2026 to help low-to moderate-income customers with past-due energy bills. The program offers a dollar-for-dollar match up to $1,000 and is designed to prevent service disconnections. Combined with the REACH program, eligible customers could receive up to $1,300 in assistance.

feed.title: Rockwell Automation
feed.summary: THG Nutrition, parent company of Myprotein, has selected Rockwell Automation's Plex Manufacturing Execution System (MES) to streamline its operations, enhance real-time visibility, and support rapid growth across its global facilities. The cloud-based solution will unify manufacturing processes, replace legacy systems, and enable THG Nutrition to standardize operations more efficiently, especially given its growth-by-acquisition business model. The implementation will begin at THG's Warrington facility, with plans for broader expansion.

feed.title: Aviation Capital Group Grows Portfolio With Order for 50 Boeing 737 MAX Aircraft
feed.summary: Aviation Capital Group (ACG) has finalized an order for 50 additional Boeing 737 MAX jets, comprising 25 737-8 and 25 737-10 variants, scheduled for delivery in 2032 and 2033. This order expands ACG's 737 MAX order book to 121 aircraft, reinforcing its commitment to fuel-efficient technology and meeting growing customer demand. The 737-10 model makes ACG the lessor with the largest backlog for Boeing's largest single-aisle jet, with all aircraft powered by LEAP-1B engines from CFM International.

feed.title: Electric off-road carts ride into AIMExpo 2026 as Kandi signs new partners
feed.summary: Kandi Technologies (NASDAQ: KNDI) participated in AIMExpo 2026, showcasing its electric off-road vehicles and signing new collaboration agreements with channel partners to deepen its North American market strategy. The company highlighted its localized operations, including a U.S. production facility and distribution centers, to support channel expansion and reiterated its focus on the North American market for sustainable long-term growth. This participation reinforces Kandi's recent strategic moves in technology and channel build-out.

feed.title: Kandi Technologies Showcases at AIMExpo 2026, Deepening Accessibility
feed.summary: Kandi Technologies Group, Inc. participated in the American International Motorcycle Expo (AIMExpo) 2026, showcasing its electric off-road vehicles and strengthening its North American channel network. The company engaged with existing dealers and new partners, formalizing collaboration agreements, and reiterating its focus on the North American market for product evolution, channel development, and localized operations. Kandi Technologies aims to enhance its industry visibility and support long-term business growth in the powersports and off-road vehicle sectors.

feed.title: Lloyds to boost North East businesses with more than £1bn in finance
feed.summary: Lloyds Banking Group has pledged over £1 billion in new finance for North East businesses in 2026, as part of a larger £35 billion investment across the UK. This commitment aims to stimulate economic growth, support local businesses like Holystone Civils, and fund major projects at Newcastle Airport and the Port of Tyne. The initiative also focuses on green growth opportunities and builds on a prior partnership between Lloyds, North East Mayor Kim McGuiness, and the North East Combined Authority to attract private sector funding.

feed.title: Markel hires Chubb’s Gureja as chief risk officer of US and Bermuda
